name: MYRIAD GENETICS INC
business: Myriad Genetics is a molecular diagnostics and precision medicine company that develops and commercializes genetic tests to assess disease risk, guide treatment decisions, and improve patient care across oncology, women's health, and mental health. Its tests are sold to healthcare providers, patients, and biopharma partners.
namedSuppliers: University of Texas M.D. Anderson Cancer Center
Mayo Foundation for Medical Education and Research

exposures.sentence: We develop and commercialize molecular tests that help patients and providers uncover genetic insights.

In early 2025, we entered into a strategic collaboration with PATHOMIQ, Inc. pursuant to which we obtained exclusive U.S. licensing rights to PATHOMIQ’s AI-enabled diagnostic platform, PATHOMIQ_PRAD, to enhance our oncology portfolio and offer AI-driven prognostic and predictive solutions for prostate cancer care.

In 2025, we completed the transition of our laboratory operations to our next-generation laboratory facilities, which are designed to enhance automation, reduce turnaround time, and improve cost efficiency across our testing portfolio.
Personalized molecular data and digital and virtual consumer trends are converging to transform traditional models of care.

catalysts.event: Discontinue sales of EndoPredict in the United States
catalysts.sentence: We currently expect to discontinue sales of EndoPredict in the United States during the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Full commercial launch of FirstGene Multiple Prenatal Screen
catalysts.sentence: We expect to further advance prenatal care with the launch of FirstGene, which began a limited-access rollout in 2025 and is expected to launch fully for commercial use in 2026.

catalysts.event: Commercial launch of AI-enabled prostate cancer diagnostic test with PATHOMIQ
catalysts.sentence: We advanced development of an AI-enabled prostate cancer diagnostic test in collaboration with PATHOMIQ Inc., with an anticipated commercial launch of the test in the first half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: More than two million new cancer cases diagnosed and more than eighteen million survivors in the United States in 2025
tamClaims.sentence: Cancer remains one of the most prevalent diseases, with more than two million new cases diagnosed, and more than eighteen million survivors, in the United States in 2025.
